info.title: AAuth Who Am I
info.description:
A minimal AAuth resource server that echoes back the identity claims a resource sees from the caller's AAuth credentials. Useful for testing identity-based access.

## Authentication flow

`GET /` has three outcomes depending on what the caller presents:

1. **No HTTP signature** â†’ `401` with `Accept-Signature` header describing the required signature scheme.
2. **`aa-agent+jwt` in `Signature-Key`** â†’ `401` with `AAuth-Requirement` header containing a freshly-minted `resource_token` the agent can exchange at its Person Server (PS) for an `auth_token`. Add `?scope=` to request identity claims beyond the default agent identity.
3. **`aa-auth+jwt` in `Signature-Key`** â†’ `200` with identity claims as JSON.

get.summary: Return identity claims (or initiate AAuth flow)
get.description:
Verifies the HTTP signature on the request and responds based on the JWT type presented in the `Signature-Key` header:

- No signature â†’ `401` + `Accept-Signature`
- `aa-agent+jwt`, no `?scope=` â†’ `200` with agent's own `sub` (and `ps` if present)
- `aa-agent+jwt`, with `?scope=` â†’ `401` + `AAuth-Requirement` (resource token minted)
- `aa-auth+jwt` with `whoami` scope â†’ `200` with identity claims

All requests must carry an HTTP Message Signature over `@method`, `@authority`, `@path`, and `signature-key` using the `jkt` key scheme.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: Space-separated list of identity scopes to include in the minted `resource_token` (e.g. `email profile`). Only valid when presenting an `aa-agent+jwt`. The `whoami` scope is always included automatically.

200.description:
Identity claims echoed back. Content depends on the JWT type presented:
- `aa-auth+jwt`: identity claims from the Person Server (all non-infrastructure claims)
- `aa-agent+jwt` with no `?scope=`: agent's own `sub` and optionally `ps`

400.description: Bad request â€” invalid scope or missing required JWT claim

401.description:
Signature missing, verification failed, or an `auth_token` is required. Inspect the response headers to determine next steps:
- `Accept-Signature` present â†’ sign your request and retry
- `Signature-Error` present â†’ your signature was malformed
- `AAuth-Requirement` present â†’ exchange the `resource-token` at your PS

Accept-Signature.description: Describes the HTTP signature scheme required. Present when no signature was found on the request.

AAuth-Requirement.description: Contains a freshly-minted `resource_token` the agent exchanges at its Person Server to obtain an `auth_token`. Present when an `aa-agent+jwt` was verified and scopes were requested.

Signature-Error.description: Describes why HTTP signature verification failed. Present when a signature was present but could not be verified.

authTokenRequired.summary: Resource token minted â€” exchange at PS

403.description: Authenticated but missing the required `whoami` scope

502.description: Could not reach issuer or Person Server

get.description: Returns metadata about this resource server in the AAuth Resource Server Metadata format. Agents discover this document to learn the resource's identity, available scopes, JWKS URI, and supported R3 vocabularies.

example.description: Echoes back the identity claims a resource sees from your AAuth credentials â€” a minimal resource for testing identity-based access.

get.description: Returns the public key(s) used by this resource server to sign `resource_token` JWTs (`aa-resource+jwt`). Person Servers use this to verify resource tokens they receive.

200.description: JWKS with the resource server's public key(s)

httpSignature.description:
HTTP Message Signatures (RFC 9421). The request must carry:
- A `Signature` header with label `sig`
- A `Signature-Input` header referencing `@method`, `@authority`, `@path`, and `signature-key`
- A `Signature-Key` header containing either an `aa-agent+jwt` or `aa-auth+jwt` compact JWT, using the `jwt=` scheme

IdentityClaims.description: Identity claims from the Person Server, after stripping JWT infrastructure claims (`iss`, `aud`, `exp`, `iat`, `jti`, `cnf`, `dwk`, `act`, `scope`). The exact set of properties depends on which scopes were granted.

sub.description: Subject identifier (person's stable ID at the PS)

AgentIdentity.description: Agent's own identity, returned when an `aa-agent+jwt` is verified but no `?scope=` was requested.

ps.description: URL of the agent's Person Server (if present in agent_token)

r3_vocabularies.description: Maps R3 vocabulary URIs to their discovery endpoints.
